def: adj. Signed; bearing signature; endorsed; under one's own hand and signature. dast-khatī zohrī , An endorsement; a writing on the back of a paper. dast-darāz ; H. hāth-lapak yā chālāk , adj. Having long arms; oppressive; extortionate. dast-darāzī , n. f. Exaction; imposition; encroachment; excess; outrage; violence; oppression. dast-darāzī k. , v. a. 1. To molest; plunder; pillage. 2. To oppress; beat; ill-treat. 3. To commit rape or an indecent assault. dast-rasī; dast-ras ; H. pahuṅch , n. f. Reach; ability; power. dast-i-shafā , A complimentary term for a physician. dast-qalam , Mah. Wom. A lettered man. dast-kār , n. m. A handicraftsman; manufacturer; an operative; artizan. dast-kārī , n. f. 1. Handwork; handicraft; manipulation; manufacture. 2. ( Fac .) Onanism; masturbation. dast-gardāṅ , 1. A short-term loan on a verbal promise to pay ( hath-udhār ). 2. Any article hawked about for sale ( biktī-huī ). Dastgardāṅ chīz aksar sastī mil jātī hai. dast-gīrī , n. f. Assistance; succour; aid; relief; protection; patronage. dast lagnā , v. n. To have loose stools.
s.m. Zinc, tutanag; lapis calaminaris.
more: s. m. Zinc, tutanag, lapis calaminaris.

def: s.m. A handle, helve, haft, hilt; a pestle; a handful, bundle; a bundle of twenty-four arrows; a quire (of paper); a hank or skein (of silk, thread, &c.), a clew; a nosegay; a flower-bed; a kind of button (sewn on the qabā or coat); division of an army, brigade, squadron
more: n. m. 1. A handle; hilt. 2. A pestle. 3. A quire of paper; a bundle of twenty-four arrows, etc. 4. A division of an army. 5. A nosegay; a bouquet. 6. A flower bed; parterre.

s. m. 1. A handle. 2. A pestle. 3. A quire of paper. 4. A bundle consisting of twenty four arrows. 5. A handful. 6. A skein of thread. 7. A division of an army, a brigade, squadron. دسته دار dasta-dār, s. m. Commander of a dasta, a brigadier.
subs: s.m. Commander of a division or brigade, a brigadier.
wety: From Classical Persian دسته (dasta, “bunch, bundle; group, cohort; handle, hilt”).

def: s.f. The strap which depends from the hilt of a sword; — a falconer's glove ( = dastakī).

s.f. 'A little hand'; a handle; a knock or rap (at a door, &c.); clapping of the hands (com. with a view of drawing a person's attention); — a pass, passport; permit, license; a summons, citation, warrant, judicial writ, process; fee for serving a summons, &c.; commission; tax
more: n. f. lit . knocking at the door. 1. Summons; judicial writ; process; legal process; warrant; revenue process, esp. a notice to pay land revenue which has fallen due. 2. A fee for serving summons, or writ, or for a passport. 3. Commission; tax ( kar ). 4. A passport; permit. Formerly, a document authorizing the free transit of certain goods and their exemption from custom dues in favor of English traders. dastak denā, kunḍī khaṭkhaṭānā , v. a. To knock at the door. dastak yā dhauṅs bāṅdhnā , v. n. To incur needless expenses ( kharch bāṅdhā ). dastak lagānā , v. n. To impose or levy a tax.
s. f. 1. Clapping of the hands, rapping at a door. 2. A pass, passport, summons, citation. 3. Commission. دستک ديني dastak denī, To knock or rap at a door.
subs: (-par), To knock (at a door, &c.); to clap the hands
(-par), To fix or attach a summons, &c. (to a door, wall, &c.); to impose or levy a tax (on).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دستک (dastak).

def: s.m. Fee for a passport, or for serving a writ or summons.
s.f. A falconer's glove; — a pocket-book; a tweezer-case.
more: s. f. 1. A pocketbook, a tweezer-case. 2. A falconerʼs glove.

def: s.f. A story, fable, tale; history; — fame, notoriety
more: n. f. A long story or tale; novel.
s. f. A story, fable, tale.
subs: A story-teller, a reciter of tales.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَاسْتَان (dāstān). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi داستاں (dastaṉ).

def: s.f. = dāstān, q.v. s.f. A story, fable, tale; history; — fame, notoriety
subs: A story-teller, a reciter of tales.

ety: dast, q.v.+suff. -āna = Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: s.m. A glove, a gauntlet; a falconer's glove; the handle of a shield.
more: n. m. A glove; muff; gauntlet ( panjah ).
s. m. A glove, a gauntlet.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دستانه (dastāna). First attested as Middle Hindi دستانہ (dastāna).

def: direct plural of داسْتان (dāstān)
n. m. A reciter of tales; a story teller.
vocative plural of دَسْتانَہ (dastāna, “glove”)
oblique plural of داسْتان (dāstān)
oblique plural of دَسْتانَہ (dastāna, “glove”)
s.f. A sash or fine muslin cloth wrapped round a turban; — a napkin, towel; a cloth, &c. (on which dishes are laid)
more: n. f. A turban ( pagṛī ). Sab ustare bāṅdho, koī talvār na bāṅdho! Kar do ye manādī 'koī dastār na bāṅdho! ' (A satire on frivolous and arbitrary regulations.) Let all men razors wear and barbers be! swords none shall wear! Let proclamation now be made, no turbans men shall wear! Dastār, raftār, guftār judī judī . Prov. Turban, and gait, and speech, all different are in each. dastār-band , n. m. One who makes up turbans. dastār-i-fazīlat, dastār-i-hikmat , n. f. The costume of a doctor of philosophy.
s. f. A turband. دستاربند dastārband, A servant whose business it is to make up the turband. دستارخوان dastār-ḵẖẉān, s. m. A tablecloth.
subs: s.m. A servant whose business it is to make up the turban
s.m. A cloth, &c. to place dishes on (vulg. dastar-ḵẖẉān, q.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَسْتَار (dastār), from دَسْت (dast) + ـَار (-ār). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi دستار (dstar /⁠dastār⁠/).

def: s. (dakh.) Tablecloth: see دستر خوان dastar-khẉān.
s.m. A small turban or sash; — a napkin; towel; handkerchief.
s.m. A table-cloth, a piece of cloth or leather, &c. spread on the ground, upon which the dishes of a meal are placed
more: n. m. A table cloth; a piece of cloth or leather, etc., spread on the ground on which the food is laid out and around which the guests squat and dine. dastar-/?/khuāṅ kī billī , n. f. The cat of the banquet, i. e . one who presents himself uninvited at a feast; a sponge.
(properly دستارخوان dastār-ḵẖẉān, q. v.) s. m. A tablecloth.
subs: 'The cat of the table-cloth'; an uninvited guest, a sponge.

def: n. f. lit . what one takes into his hand. A deed; document; bond ( tamassuk ); voucher; an instrument. dast-āvez-i-jālī , n. f. A forged document. dast-āvez-i-shikastgī-i-shirākat ; H. sīrtoṛ likhtam . Deed of dissolution of partnership. dast-āvez-i-zamānat . A bail bond; a security bond. dast-āvez qābil-i-baٴi o shira . A negotiable instrument. dast-āvez-i-lā-dٴavī , A deed of renunciation. dast-āvez-i-mahr, kābīn-nāmah . A deed of dower. dast-āvez nīlāmī , An auction-sale deed. dast āvez-i-hiba , A deed of gift or bequest. dast ba-khair , intj. Safe be this hand! (Said while pointing out on one's own body the seat of another's malady or pain). dast-ba-dast ; H. hāthoṅ-hāth , adv. 1. From hand to hand. 2. Quick; expeditious. dast-ba-dast ānā , v. n. To pass from hand to hand or from generation to generation. dast-ba-dast muāmlā ; H. hāthoṅ hāth len den . A ready-money purchase or transaction. dast-burd, gaban , n. f. Encroachment; usurpation; embezzlement; plunder; rapine. dast-burd k. , v. a. To embezzle; plunder; usurp. dast-bardār h. , v. n. To give up; wash one's hands of ( tajnā , 1, 2.). dast-bardārī, lit . drawing back the hand. Withdrawal; relinquishment; renunciation. dast-basta ; H. hāth-joṛe , adv. With folded hands. dast-basta h. , v. n. To wait on a person with folded hands; to attend; wait on. dast-buqcha , n. m. Cloth in which a small bundle is wrapped. dast-band , n. m. A string of pearls or precious stones worn on the wrist. dast-bandhak , n. f. A pledge; deposit. be-dast o pā , n. m. 1. A cripple. 2. One who is destitute or helpless. jhūṭī dast-āvez banānā , v. a. To make a false document. ham-dast, ba-dast , prep. Through; by; with; per ; under an escort or convoy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَسْتَاویز (dastāwēz).

def: s.f. See s.v. dast. s.m. Hand (syn. hāth); a cubit; — suit (of armour or clothes); anything complete (as a house with its offices); purging or loose stool, stool, purge, evacuation
subs: adj. & s.m. Extending or moving the hands in dancing, or in relinquishing anything, or in anger or excitement, or in denying; — one who moves the hands in dancing, &c.
adj. 'Accustomed to the hand,' tame, familiarized, gentle, tractable; — handy; skilful, dexterous
v.n. To have diarrhœa or loose bowels
adj. & s.m. Intermeddling, interfering; — one who interferes, &c.; one who commences a business; — an encroacher; an oppressor, a plunderer
s.f. Putting the hand (to, -meṅ, anything), meddling (with), interference; encroachment; oppression
(-meṅ), To put the hand (to), to meddle, &c.

s.f. 'That which ties or fastens the hands'; what a man takes with him as a means of promoting his suit; what one gets into his hand and depends on; a signature; a note of hand, bond, deed, title-deed, voucher, certificate, instrument, charter, &c.; — a small present to be given into the hands of a person whose favour is sought; — instance or example at hand
May good attend the hand (said by one who points out on his own body the seat of another's malady or pain)

To put up the hands in prayer
s.m. Pre-excellence, superiority, advantage, victory (at play, in war or law); encroachment, usurpation; plunder, rapine; misappropriation, embezzlement

adv. With close or folded hands (in token of respect)

s.m. Cloth in which a small bundle is wrapped up
s.m. A string of pearls or precious stones worn (by women) on the wrist; — a gold or silver ornament worn on the back of the hand
To kiss the hand, to salute
s.f. Lightness of finger; stealth; — a civil war; a duel:-dast-ḵẖat̤, vulg. daskhat, daskat, s.m. 'Hand-writing'; signature; endorsement, or acceptance; — a manuscript, chirography
adj. Written with one's own hand; in manuscript; signed
adj. & s.m. 'Long-armed'; oppressive, extortionate; — an oppressor
s.f. Violence, oppression, extortion, exaction, excess
(-se, or -par?), To practice violence or oppression, to oppress, ill-treat, plunder, &c.

adj. Within (one's) reach or power; to be reached with the hand; able; come to hand, obtained; — s.f. Reach; ability, power, means; aid, assistance; facility; dexterity, skill; acquisition, attainment
v.n. To come to hand, to arrive; to be reached; to be obtained; to have in one's power, to be able
'Having healing in his hand,' an epithet of a physician who is successful in restoring his patients to health
s.m. lit. 'The hidden hand'; a charm or incantation by means of which a hidden hand supplies one's wants
s.m. A retailer, a hawker, a pedlar
s.f. Pedlary; the business of a pedlar
adj. Amputated in the hand; — s.m. A writer
adj. Wrought, manufactured; dexterous, expert, expeditious; — s.m. One who lives by the work of his hands, an artificer, handicraftsman, artizan, artist, a manufacturer
s.f. Manufacture, handicraft; art, trade; operation (in surgery); dexterity
adj. & s.m. Taken by the hand; led; — oppressed, weak; — a led horse; a guide (of the blind), a leader; a beggar; a captive
or dast-gah, s.f. Power, strength, ability, means; understanding; intellect; knowledge
adj. & s.m.? Going from hand to hand; hawked about; — money, &c. which is obtained on loan, a loan for a short period (on a verbal promise to pay); — anything hawked about for sale
(-ko), To lend (money, &c.) for a short period
adj. & s.m. Taken by the hand, helped; — one who is taken by the hand, a protégé
adj. & s.m. Taking by the hand; laying hands on; imprisoned; — a helper, assistant, patron, protector, a guardian-saint; — a prisoner
s.f. Assistance, aid, help; defence, support, protection, patronage
s.m.? The first money received for sale of merchandize in the morning, handsel (syn. bonī)

dast-nigarān, adj. lit. 'Regarding the hand'; needy, wanting, in want
'To have the hand on the collar,' to engage in combat
adj. Attained, obtained; reached; attainable, procurable; — dast-yāb honā, v.n. To be attained, be acquired, be found, &c.; to attain (to), reach
s.m. A bracelet (of gold or silver).

adj. Of or from the hand, manual; — having a handle, handled; — s.f. A small handle; a vessel with a handle or handles; any vessel, &c., that may be lifted by or carried in the hand; a small pen-case; a torch or link carried in the hand; a trick with the hand (in wrestling); a present to native officials at the dasahrā festival.
more: adj. Of or from the hands; manual. dastī , n. f. 1. A small handle. 2. A small pen-case. 3. A torch or link carried in the hands. 4. A hand-trick in wrestling. 5. A present to native officials at the Dasehra festival.
s. f. A torch or link that is carried in the hands. adj. Of or from the hand, manual.

def: handicraft, art, expertise; dexterity; operation (in surgery)

def: n. m. Tongs ( chimṭā ).
Come to hand, obtained.

ety: dast, q.v.+ūr = var = Zend bar = S. भर्
def: s.m. A prime-minister ( = vazīr); a councillor, a senator a model, exemplar, rule, regulation; basis, foundation settled or established order, custom, usage, practice, fashion, mode, manner; a customary fee or tax, or percentage; — a district, an aggregate of parganas
more: n. m. Z. dastobar ; P. dast hand, bar take. 1. A common practice ( chāl , 4, 5, 7.). 2. Settled or established order. 3. A customary fee or perquisite; a commission or percentage on the collections. dastūr-i-tijārat, yā mahājanī , Mercantile or commercial usage. dastūr jārī h. , v. n. To be established (a rule or practice). dastūr-i-khāndān , n. m. Family usage. dastūr-i-mustamara . An invariable practice. dastūr-i-mulk . Local usage; the usage of the country. dastūr muāfiq , adv. According to custom; as before; ordinarily; customarily. ba-dastūr , adv. According to rule ( hasb-i-dastūr ); as heretofore; as before; in statu quo. be-dastūr , adj. 1. Unprecedented; novel; anomalous (انريت 1.). 2. Irregular; informal; unlawful.
s. m. 1. Custom, fashion, usage, mode, manner. 2. A minister, senator. 3. Model, regulation. دستورالعمل dastūru-l-aﻌmal, s. m. A rule, regulation, model.
subs: s.m. Model, rule, regulation; rules of practice, body of instructions, manual of regulations, hand-book, manual; code of laws; constitution, form of government
adv. According to custom, &c. ( = next, q.v.)
adv. According to rule or custom, as usual, as heretofore, in statu quo
adj. Without example or precedent, unprecedented, irregular, informal, anomalous, unlawful.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَسْتُور (dastūr).

def: s.m. pl. (of dastūr, after the model of the Ar.), Customs, usages, practices, modes; customary perquisites, fees, per-centage.

adj. Customary, usual, ordinary; — s.f. Perquisites paid to servants by one who sells to their master (usually at the rate of one or two pice in the rupee), perquisites, fees, commission of a broker; — leave, license, congé
more: n. f. 1. Perquisites; commission of a broker; perquisites paid to servants by one who sells to their master. Zauq meṅ shauq, dastūrī meṅ laṛkā . Prov. The pair had pleasure rare, a child thrown in the bargain as it were. 2. Emoluments; allowances. dastūr'iyā , n. m. A broker.
1. s. f. Perquisites paid to servants by one who sells to their master. 2. Leave, license, congé. 3. adj. Customary, ordinary. دستوري مين dastūrī meṅ, In ordinary, in the customary way.
subs: adv. In the customary way, in ordirary.

s.f. Violence, oppression, extortion, exaction, excess
(-se, or -par?), To practice violence or oppression, to oppress, ill-treat, plunder, &c.

adj. Within (one's) reach or power; to be reached with the hand; able; come to hand, obtained; — s.f. Reach; ability, power, means; aid, assistance; facility; dexterity, skill; acquisition, attainment
v.n. To come to hand, to arrive; to be reached; to be obtained; to have in one's power, to be able
'Having healing in his hand,' an epithet of a physician who is successful in restoring his patients to health
s.m. lit. 'The hidden hand'; a charm or incantation by means of which a hidden hand supplies one's wants
s.m. A retailer, a hawker, a pedlar
s.f. Pedlary; the business of a pedlar
adj. Amputated in the hand; — s.m. A writer
adj. Wrought, manufactured; dexterous, expert, expeditious; — s.m. One who lives by the work of his hands, an artificer, handicraftsman, artizan, artist, a manufacturer
s.f. Manufacture, handicraft; art, trade; operation (in surgery); dexterity
adj. & s.m. Taken by the hand; led; — oppressed, weak; — a led horse; a guide (of the blind), a leader; a beggar; a captive
or dast-gah, s.f. Power, strength, ability, means; understanding; intellect; knowledge
adj. & s.m.? Going from hand to hand; hawked about; — money, &c. which is obtained on loan, a loan for a short period (on a verbal promise to pay); — anything hawked about for sale
(-ko), To lend (money, &c.) for a short period
adj. & s.m. Taken by the hand, helped; — one who is taken by the hand, a protégé
adj. & s.m. Taking by the hand; laying hands on; imprisoned; — a helper, assistant, patron, protector, a guardian-saint; — a prisoner
s.f. Assistance, aid, help; defence, support, protection, patronage
s.m.? The first money received for sale of merchandize in the morning, handsel (syn. bonī)
(ko), To feel a desire to ease nature; to have loose stools
s.m. lit. 'Hand-wiper'; a towel; napkin; handkerchief
dast-nigarān, adj. lit. 'Regarding the hand'; needy, wanting, in want
'To have the hand on the collar,' to engage in combat
adj. Attained, obtained; reached; attainable, procurable; — dast-yāb honā, v.n. To be attained, be acquired, be found, &c.; to attain (to), reach
s.m. A bracelet (of gold or silver).

def: s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. Bathing on the tenth day after child-birth.
n. m. H. das ten, uṭhnā to rise. Ablution performed by a Hin. mother on the tenth day after childbirth.

ety: Prk. दसमओ, or दसवँओ; S. दश+तम+कः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -wīṅ), Tenth; — a tenth part, a tenth; — the ten days during which alms, &c. are given for the benefit of the soul of a deceased relative; prayers or offerings on the tenth day (after a death, &c.).

adj. = daswāṅ, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -wīṅ), Tenth; — a tenth part, a tenth; — the ten days during which alms, &c. are given for the benefit of the soul of a deceased relative; prayers or offerings on the tenth day (after a death, &c.).

adj. 1. The tenth, or tenth part. 2. The ten days during which alms are given and the brotherhood fed daily for the benefit of the soul of the deceased which is believed to dwell in the house during this period. On the tenth night Shastars are read by a Pandit all night.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi दसवै (dasavai), दसवैं (dasava͠i, Rājasthānī manuscripts) (with adverbializing affix -ai), from Prakrit 𑀤𑀲𑀫 (dasama), from Sanskrit दशम (daśamá). Doublet of دَہُم (dahum, dahm). By surface analysis, دَس (das) + ـوَاں (vā̃).

def: s.m. A tenth part (added to the revenue, &c.).
s.f. Tenth day of a month; — tenth day of the lunar fortnight ( = dasamī).
s.m. Slavery, servitude, service.
s.m. A cruel or mischievous man; an enemy; an oppressor, wrong-doer; a thief, robber, bandit, ruffian; an outcast; name of one of the mixed classes
subs: s.f. Dishonest mode of living, theft, &c.

def: s.m. Bountifulness, liberality.
more: n. f. 1. A stopper; a cork; spike; wadding. 2. An arch; a key-stone; a vault. ḍāṭ ḍap/?/ṭ , n. f. Brow-beating; snub; check. ḍāṭ lag nā , v. a. To cork; put a stopper on.
s. m. Bountifulness. ¶ For دانت dānt, q. v.

def: adj. (dakh.) Thick (as, a wood), close; thicket, arbour.
s.m. corr. of dāṅt, q.v. s.m. Tooth; tusk (of an elephant, or a boar)
subs: To bite the fore-finger in astonishment, or amazement, or perplexity, &c.; to indicate surprise, &c.
v.n. The teeth to chatter; — to squabble, wrangle; — chattering of the teeth; squabbling, &c.
To show the teeth, &c. ( = dāṅt nikālnā or nikosnā, qq.v.)
v.n. The teeth to be set or clenched (as in lockjaw, &c.)
v.t. To detract from the virtues or praise of (another)
v.n. The teeth to chatter, &c. ( = dāṅt bājnā)
To gnash the teeth (in anger, &c.); to grin
dāṅt-tale uṅglī dabānā or kāṭnā = dāṅt uṅglī kāṭnā
and dāṅtoṅ-meṅ uṅglī denā; — dāṅt tinkā, The taking a straw or a piece of grass in the mouth to express submission, or to deprecate anger (see dāṅtoṅ-meṅ tinkā lenā)
(-ke), To break the teeth (of); to render powerless, to humble, to bring (one) low (cf. Eng. 'to draw one's teeth')
(-ke), 'To knock out the teeth (of)'; to make one laugh the wrong side of his mouth
v.n. The teeth to fall out, or to be broken
(-par), To desire exceedingly, to covet (a thing); to be bent (on a thing, as revenge, &c.)
(-kī), 'To eat bread bitten by another'; to be an intimate friend (of), be in close friendship (with)
or kaćkaćānā, or kićkićānā, To grind the teeth, to gnash the teeth (in rage, &c.) = dāṅt pīsnā
(-ke), To set the teeth on edge; to excite the envy or jealousy (of); to dishearten, discourage; to displease; to baffle, to disappoint, to frustrate
(-ke), The teeth to be set on edge; to have (one's) cupidity, or envy, &c. excited; to be displeased, &c.
s.f. A preparation of wheat, poppy-seed, and sugar (a ceremony observed by Mohammadans on the appearance of a child's first tooth)
and dāṅt lānā, v.n. = dāṅt nikalnā, q.v.
(-par), To fix or set the teeth (upon)
v.n. The teeth to be set or clenched (as in lockjaw = dāṅt baiṭhnā)
(-par), To bite; — (met.) to acquire or get possession (of a thing), to possess (oneself) of
dāṅt nikāsnā, or nikosnā, or nikālnā, To show the teeth, to grin, to laugh; to express or confess inability or helplessness, to whine, to cringe
dāṅt nikalnā, v.n. To teeth; to grow (as teeth)
(-par), The teeth to be fixed (on); to desire eagerly, &c. ( = dāṅt rakhnā, q.v.)
(-ke), 'To have no tartar on the teeth' (through fasting); to be starving, be in poverty or destitution
v.n. To be cutting the teeth, be teething
(-ke), 'To come between the teeth (of)'; to provoke the envy or hatred (of)
'To seize the ground with the teeth'; to be reduced to extremities; to be in great pain
To be utterly powerless to keep (anything) in (one's) grasp
To gnash the teeth ( = dāṅt pīsnā)
To bite the fore-finger in indication of astonishment, or perplexity, or shame, or remorse, or sorrow, &c.; to stand amazed, be astonished, to wonder; to feel remorse, &c.
'To take a straw or a piece of grass in the mouth'; to express submission (to another), or to deprecate the anger of (the action implying, 'I am your cow, and therefore entitled to your protection')
(or zabān) honā, 'To be with the tongue between the teeth'; to live unmolested or uninjured in the midst of enemies
'The milk-teeth to be shed'; to be no longer a child, to have gained wisdom and experience (e.g. dūdh-ke dāṅt abhī nahīṅ ṭūṭe, 'he is yet unwise and inexperienced').

def: s.f. Restraining, restraint, checking, check, snubbing, brow-beating, threatening, threat, menacing gesture; — a stopper, stopple, cork, plug, spigot, spike; key-stone (of an arch; cf. ḍāṅṭ or ḍāṅth, ḍāṅṭhal, ḍāṅṭhī, ḍaṭṭā, ḍanthal)
more: (from ڐاٿنا) s. f. Threatening, snubbing, browbeating, checking.
subs: s.f. Threat, menacing gesture; attack, assault, charge, rush or gallop (upon or against; cf. ḍhāṭī)
(-meṅ), To put a stopple or plug, &c. (in), to plug, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Giving, bestowing, imparting; liberal, generous; — giver, donor, benefactor, a liberal man
more: n. m. 1. Giver; donor; a charitable person. 1. Dātā kī nāo pahāṛ chaṛhe . Prov. The liberal man's boat sails up-hill. 2. Dātā datār, sutnī utār . Prov. (My husband) is so liberal, he would even take off my trousers and give them away. 3. Hār jīt sab māṅ rahe, hāre nāh datār . Rus. Prov. Loss and gain come to all, but the liberal man never loses. 2. Benefactor; patron. Dāte! yād Allāh! Mendicants' mutual salutation. O great one! remember God! 3. ( dātā ) God; Creator; Almighty God. Dātā ke tīn gun, de, na de, de-ke phir chhīn le . Prov. God has three attributes, he gives, he withholds, he takes away what he has given.
adj. Liberal, generous; subst. Giver, benefactor, donor.

def: s.f. = dāṅtan, datwan, qq.v. s.m. A piece of wood, or a twig (com. of the nīm, or the babūl tree), used as a tooth-brush; — (in Braj) pl. of dāṅt, q.v.
subs: To brush the teeth, and rinse the mouth.

def: v.t. To restrain, curb, rein in, stop, check, make stationary, &c. (see ḍaṭnā, and ḍāṭnā).
v.t. = dabkānā, q.v. v.t. To stow away, to conceal, hide; — to check, chide, snub, threaten, browbeat, daunt, awe, cow, intimidate.

ety: pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं
def: s.m. Liberality, beneficence; benevolence.
s.m. = dātā.
contrac. of dātār, q.v. (used in comp.).
s.m. A large rake (used for gathering high grass together into a cocklet).
adj. & s.m. To be given or bestowed; — anything that may be, or ought to be, given.
s.f. Liberality, generosity
s.m. A stalk, &c. ( = ḍāṅṭh, ḍanṭhā, &c.).

ety: fr. dat = S. दन्त; or akin to ḍāṭnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To face, confront; to oppose, to fight, to attack; to confute.
more: v. n. S. तिष्ठन् See ṭhairnā , 1, 3-6, 8, and jamnā 1-3.
v. a. To oppose, to confute, to fight.

def: v.t. = datnā, q.v.; — v.n. To stop; to be confronted. v.t. To face, confront; to oppose, to fight, to attack; to confute.

ety: ḍāṭ˚ = Prk. उट्ठ(इ), fr.डट्ठ = S. दष्ट
def: v.t. To restrain, curb, check, rein in (a horse, &c.); to chide, rebuke, threaten, snub, brow-beat; to correct, chastise, punish; — to stop up, to plug; to fill (as a ćilam, &c.), to stuff, cram ( = ṭhūṅsnā).
more: v. a. To snub, check, chide, threaten, to browbeat. See ڐانٿنا ḍānṭnā.

def: v. n. 1. To cram ( ṭhūṅsnā , 1.). 2. To fill the chilam , q. v. Sulfā ḍāṭo! 3. To check; snub; browbeat; deter. 1. Kāṭe pe kadhī phare, koṭī yatan koū sīṅch, Binai na mān, Khagesh, sun, ḍāṭehī pe nav nīch . Rāmāyan. Howe'er you water it, uncut the plantain will not bear, To all entreaty deaf, Khagesh, harsh language cows him e'er. 2. Arī napūtī! apṛeṅ ne ḍāṭ ? Rus. Wom. You wretch! why dont you check your children? 4. To correct; chastize; punish. 5. To pull the rein; to rein in.

v.n. To be restrained, be checked; to stop, stand still, be or become stationary; to halt, stay, remain: to be or become fixed or firm; to last, endure; to be settled or agreed on; to be determined or resolved.
more: v. n. To stop, to stand still.

def: s.f. Land which has been lately cropped with the Indian corn, or bājrā, or joʼār.
s.m. = dātā-pan, q.v. s.m. Liberality, beneficence; benevolence.
s.m. An instrument for cutting or chopping; a sort of sickle; a large knife, a bill-hook, or hatchet (see dā).
adj. & s.m. = dātā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Giving, bestowing, imparting; liberal, generous; — giver, donor, benefactor, a liberal man
s.f. = S داترتو दातृत्व dātr̤itva, s.m. Beneficence, bountifulness, liberality.
s.m. = S داترتا दातृता dātr̤itā, s.f. Beneficence, bountifulness, liberality.
s.f. A girl who is given in marriage.
more: n. f. A damsel given in marriage. Bate .
E. n. m. A cork; stopper; plug.

def: s.m. = ḍaṭṭā, q.v. part. & s.m. (f. -ā), Given, granted, presented, made over, assigned; — a common name or title of a man of the Vaiśya caste, and also of some Kāyasths
more: s. (dakh.) A cork, a gag ( Harris ): see ڐٿا ḍaṭṭā.
subs: (˚ta+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Self-given; — one who has given himself; a youth or orphan who gives himself to persons disposed to adopt him

def: s.m. A stopper, stopple, cork, plug, spigot ( = ḍāṭ); a fence.
more: s. m. A plug, spigot, stopple, cork.

def: part. & s.m. (f. -ā), Given, granted, presented, made over, assigned; — a common name or title of a man of the Vaiśya caste, and also of some Kāyasths
subs: (˚ta+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Self-given; — one who has given himself; a youth or orphan who gives himself to persons disposed to adopt him

def: s.m. A son given away by his natural parents to persons engaging to adopt him (one of the twelve kinds of heirs acknowledged by the old Hindū law).
s.f. Gift, donation, offering.
s.f. A match.
s.f. = 1˚ datvan, q.v.; — 2˚ Cleaning the teeth with a toothbrush.
s.m. A tooth-brush (com. a piece of stick or a twig of the nīm or babūl tree).
s.m. An appellation of a father or of an elder brother; — Baladeva, Kr̤ishṇa's elder brother.
more: n. m. S. दाऊ to cut. A sickle; billhook; a kind of hatchet with the point curved.
n. m. S. दैव deity. An appellation of an elder brother; Baldeva, Krishnā's elder brother.

def: s. m. 1. An appellation of a father or of an elder brother. 2. (used by contraction for) Baladeva, Kr̤ish̤ṇaʼs elder brother. ¶ 3. (dakh.) Deceit, fraud: see دغا dag̠ẖā.
s. f. Toil, &c. See دوادو.
Dawood, Dawud, David; a male given name from Arabic
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian داوُد (dāwud), from Arabic دَاوُد (dāwud).

def: (Heb. from דוד Beloved) n. prop. Name of a prophet, David.
s.f. Name of a kind of white wheat; name of a sort of garland.
1. s. f. A shrub that bears a flower like chamomile (Chrysanthemum indicum); a kind of firework like that shrub. 2. A kind of armour. 3. adj. Relating to David, of David.

adj. Relating to David, of David; — s.f. Chain armour (so called because it is believed to have been originally made by David), coat of mail; — name of a shrub that bears a flower like camomile, Chrysanthemum indicum; a firework in imitation of this shrub; — a kind of white wheat ( = dāʼūd-ḵẖānī).
more: adj. Relating to David; of David. dāūdī ; Pop. dāodī , n. f. 1. A shrub that bears a flower like chamomile; Chrysanthemum indicum . 2. A firework in imitation of this shrub. 3. ( Dāvat-khānī ) a kind of white wheat. 4. A kind of armour. (?).

def: s.m. Daughter's son, grandson.
s.f. Daughter's daughter, granddaughter.

r: daʻuī, daʻwạ̄, daʻwā ḵẖārij karnā, kā, daʻwe-dār, daʻwī-dār
def: s.m. A pretender; a claimant, suitor, plaintiff
subs: (with gen. of thing, and loc., -par, of person on whom claim is made), To lay claim (to), to claim, make a claim (on, -par, for, -kā), to sue; to make pretensions (to), to pretend, assert, profess.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَعْویٰ (da'wā), from Arabic دَعْوَى (daʕwā).

def: s.m. Shape, form, fashion, figure, make, build; appearance, aspect; natural form or state, nature, constitution; course, way, method, manner, mode, style, air, general features or character (of speech, &c.); pattern, model, specimen; opportunity; device, scheme, plan; estimate of revenue or assets (as a guide to assessment); raised boundary (of a field, &c.), boundary, limit ( = ḍaulā; cf. ḍhār); — coition, copulation ( = ḍaulā)
more: s. m. 1. Manner, method, mode. 2. Shape, fashion, form.
subs: s.m. A model-form, a blank form or return (to be used as a model)
(-ko), To bring into shape, give shape or form to, put in order, regulate, systematize
(-kā), To set the example (of), lay the foundation (of)
adv. In due form, in order, ship-shape, methodically, &c.
adj. Without shape or form, shapeless, irregular, ill-shaped, ill-formed, ill-made, ugly, deformed; uncouth, ill-mannered, cross-grained.

def: s.m. Manner, &c.; boundary; coition (prob. fr. ḍol-nā).

ety: for A. دولة, inf. n. of un. of دول 'to come round,' &c.
def: s.f. Good fortune, prosperity, happy state or condition, happiness, felicity; riches, wealth; — state, government, monarchy, empire, sovereignty, dominion, rule; — cause, occasion, effect, means
more: n. f. دول changing (as fortune). 1. Riches; fortune ( darb ). 1. Daulat kā khel hai! Prov. All things are the sport of fortune. 2. Mohr ab koëloṅ pe hone lagī, Daulat-i-husn jab luṭā baiṭhe . Rind. She stamps but coals where erst she sealed great bags of gold, Her wealth of beauty robbed, the ruin you behold. (Bags and chests containing money are usually sealed). 3. Maiṅ mar jāūṅgī, tau kyā tū daulat par baiṭh jāëgī! Wom. 2. Empire; sovereignty; dominion; rule. Daulat-i-inglishiyā. nau-daulat, nau-daultā , A novus homo ; an upstart; a man of yesterday; mushroom gentry.

(from دول) s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. Happiness, felicity, riches, wealth. 2. Fortune, prosperity; empire, state or government. دولتخانه daulat-ḵẖāna, s. m. (house of fortune) A palace, a house (your house). دولتخانه خاص daulat-ḵẖāna-e-ḵẖāṣṣ, A royal residence, the kingʼs palace. دولت خواه daulat-ḵẖẉāh, Wishing prosperity or empire, well-wisher, loyal. دولتخواهي daulat-ḵẖẉāh-ī, s. f. The wishing happiness or prosperity or empire, good wishes, good will, loyalty. دولت سرا daulat-sarā, s. f. House, palace (your house, as a respectful mode of address). دولتمند daulat-mand, adj. Wealthy, opulent, rich, fortunate. دولتمندي daulat-mand-ī, s. f. Wealthiness, opulence, prosperity, happiness. 3. Cause, occasion, effect, means.
subs: s.m. lit. 'House of fortune'; a mansion, palace, house (in polite speech, e.g. āp-kā daulat-ḵẖāna kahāṅ hai, 'Where is your house?')
A royal residence, the king's palace
adj. & s.m. Wishing happiness or prosperity or empire, loyal; — a well-wisher, a loyal subject (com. applied by the writer of a letter or petition to himself in the sense of 'I' or 'me')
s.f. The wishing happiness or prosperity or empire, good wishes, goodwill, loyalty
s.m. Seat of good fortune, abode of felicity; seat of sovereignty
adj. & s.m. Fortunate, prosperous, happy; wealthy, opulent, rich; — a rich person, &c.
v.t. To bless or prosper (with, -se), to enrich (with)
s.f. Prosperity, happiness; wealthiness, wealth, opulence
s.m. Possessor of wealth, a rich man
(-kī), postpn. By the good fortune (of), by means (of), by the favour (of), through
s.m. A novus homo, a parvenu, an upstart.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَولَت (dawlat), borrowed from Arabic دَوْلَة (dawla).

def: n. m. lit . house of fortune. A mansion (a polite term for another's dwelling-house). Āp kā daulat-khānā kahāṅ hai? daulat-khāna-i-khās , The king's palace; the royal residence.
direct plural of دَولَت (daulat)
adj. & s.m. = daulat-mand, q.v.s.v. daulat.

adj. Wealthy; rich; opulent. daulat-mand, daulat-vālā, daultā, daultī , n. m. A wealthy person. 1. Kyā garaz lākh khudāī meṅ hoṅ daulat-vāle, Unkā bandah hūṅ jobande haiṅ muhabbat-vāle . Zauq. I care not lacs in gold or worldly goods to have, A slave I am to her who is affection's slave. 2. Eī daultī apne tehe meṅ āp khaultī . Wom. Prov. Madam purse-proud boils in her own flames.
n. f. Wealth; opulence.
oblique plural of دَولَت (daulat)
s.f. A forest-conflagration; — fire, flame; burning heat, excess of heat or thirst
subs: (-kī), To feel burning heat; to be consumed with the flame of love, to long or pine (for).

ety: S. दा 'protection'+मञ्चं, and मञ्चकं; — cf. ḍāmćā
def: s.m. An elevated platform or shed raised on bamboos in a field or orchard (where a watchman is stationed to protect the crop from cattle, birds, &c.); a scaffold, platform.
s.m. A scaffold, &c. ( = ḍaunjā, q.v.).

ety: S. दा 'protection'+मञ्चं, and मञ्चकं; — cf. ḍāmćā
def: s.m. An elevated platform or shed raised on bamboos in a field or orchard (where a watchman is stationed to protect the crop from cattle, birds, &c.); a scaffold, platform.

ety: inf. n. of دور 'to turn round, to revolve,' & c.
def: s.m. Going round, moving in a circle, revolving; revointion (of a body, or of time); circular motion; the going round, or circulating (of wine); the cup handed round; the coming round in turn (of days or times); vicissitude; — repetition (of a lesson); a kind of argument, reasoning in a circle; — circumference, perimeter; circular enclosure; border (of a garment, &c.); circle, circuit; orbit; circuit of rule, compass, jurisdiction, power, authority, dominion, sway; — a period of years, time, age, cycle; a turn, tour, round, course, progress; a turn or twist (of a rope, &c.)
more: n. m. 1. Going round; circular motion; circulation; circuit. Sharāb kā daur . Passing the wine. 2. Revolution of time or of a heavenly body. 3. Course; career; progress; process. 4. Age; period; cycle. 5. Reasoning in a circle. 6. Repetition of a lesson, especially of the Qurān. 7. Border; margin. Shāl kā daur banā do. daur-daurā , n. m. Reign; rule; empire; sway. 1. Āj kal Aṅgrezoṅ kā daur daurā hai . Now-a-days the English are the dominant race. 2. Sadā daur daurā dikhātā nahīṅ, Gayā vaqt phir hāth ātā nahīṅ . The color of the time the same will not remain, The time that's gone will not come back to you again. daur k. , v. a. To repeat lessons to one another. Āo! Qurān kā daur kar leṅ!

n. m. H. ḍor a string. 1. The strings attached to a basket to throw up water for irrigation. 2. In Panjabī, an earthen basin ( nāṅd ). daur'ā , n. m. A large basket. daur'ī , n. f. 1. A long string with fish-hooks attached, used in fishing. 2. A small basket.

n. f. S. धीर to run. 1. ( Comp .) Running; race Ghuṛ-dauṛ . 2. Reach; compass; distance; extent. Mullāṅ kī dauṛ masīt . Prov. The priest sees no farther than the mosque. 3. Endeavour; effort; struggle. 4. Incursion; aggression; attack; assault; raid. 1. Dauṛ chaṛhā lāyā . 2. Mujrim ke ghar par dauṛ bhejī gaī. dauṛ dhūp , n. f. Dauṛ dhūp karegā so hī muqadmah jītegā. dauṛ-kar chal'nā , v. n. To walk fast. Dauṛ-kar chale na rapaṭ paṛe. dauṛ mārnā , v. n. 1. To make a long quick march. 2. A basket in my hand, and kauṛis in my waist, a child to carry in my arms! 2. Kunjṛā kahtā, 'sun rī, ranḍī, ham bechā bhar daurī, Mere pās hai ḍhebuā paisā, tere pās nahīṅ kaurī, Sāg sab kahāṅ par pheṅkā?' Kunjṛauoṅ kā jhagṛā. The grocer says, 'hear, woman! I have sold a basket full, I've pice, and you have none, where are your potherbs all?'

v. n. Pālī, dhāvati . 1. To run; go fast; make haste; run a race; gallop. 1. Andhe ne chor pakṛā, dauṛiyo, be laṅgṛo! Prov. A blind man caught a thief, 'run to my help,' says he to the lame man! 2. Muflis koī bulāve to muṅh ko chhupāte haiṅ, Shakkar kā halvā sunte hī bas dauṛe jāte haiṅ , Nazīr. Kahte hue ye dil meṅ, 'ahā-hā! rī Shabbārāt! ' When poor men ask them they just turn away with some device, But when they hear of candied sweets they run all in a trice, And in their hearts exclaim 'this Shabbarāt is oh! so nice!' 2. To rush at; fall upon; attack. 3. To spread; circulate. 1. Khabar dauṛ gaī . 2. Sārebadan meṅ zahar dauṛ gayā. dauṛ dauṛ ānā , v. n. 1. To come running. 2. v. n. (dakh.) To run: see دوڙنا dauṛnā.
v. n. To run violently, to use great exertion, to toil.

ety: v.n. fr. دوي 'to be diseased'; or fr. داوي, 'to treat medically,' conj. iii of دوي
def: s.f. Medicine; a remedy
more: n. f. دو became diseased. 1. A medicine ( dārū ). 2. A remedy; cure. 1. Ādmī kī davā ūdmi hai . Prov. Man's remedy is man. 2. Khāte hī zahr mar gaë yāṅ, vāṅ khabar nahīṅ, Kyā hai asar davā meṅ! duā meṅ asar nahīṅ . Saiyad. Take poison and you're dead! she even knows it not, The drug's work's swift and sure, the yield of pray'r is naught! 3. Roz roz kī davā bhī gizā ho jātī hai . Prov. The medicine you take from day to day becomes your daily food, your life's main stay. 4. Juṛ'ī nāhīn dhur kī ṭūṭī, Dharī rahe sab dārū būṭī . Rus. Prov. Life's thread Heaven-broken ne'er can mended be, Your roots and herbs as well unused may lie. 5. Davā kā asar tīsre din hotā hai . The effects of medicine are felt on the third day. (Native physicians).
n. m. A large wooden spoon.

subs: adj. & s.m. Web-footed; leather-legged; — name of a people in India who have thin and ductile legs with which they strangle unwary travellers (they are said to pretend to be lame, and to importune travellers to carry them on their backs, which proves fatal to those whose compassion induces them to comply, as the villains twist their legs round their necks and instantly strangle them), the Thags; — a member of that class, a Thag, q.v.

def: s.m. pl. (of dūlāb = P. dol-āb), Wheels, water-wheels.
s.m. Continuing, lasting; persevering; continuance; perpetuity; duration; — adv. Continually, always, perpetually, eternally
more: (from دوم) 1. s. m. Perpetuity, duration, persevering. 2. adv. Always, eternally. علي الدوام aﻌlạ-d-dawām, adv. Continually, assiduously, incessantly.

def: s. (dakh.) 1. Lap. 2. A couple (for dogs: Harris ).
s.m. corr. of dāman, q.v.
s.m. The act or process of treading out corn (cf. dāʼeṅ); — destruction.
adj. (dialec.) Left (hand or side).

def: part. adj. Running, walking fast.
s. f. See داواگن.

ety: for davanda, act. part. of davīdan, and = S. धाव+अन्त्
def: part. adj. & s.m. Running; — a fast runner; a fleet horse, a racer.
more: part. act. (from دويدن) A runner, a racer.

def: adj. See ڐانو نڐول.

adj. = ḍāṅwāṅ-ḍol, q.v. adj. Wandering without house or home, errant; unsettled, uneasy, restless, distressed (in mind); ruined, lost, destitute; — s.m. A running or hurrying to and fro; a convulsion (as of an earthquake, &c.)
subs: v.n. To go wandering about, to be unsettled, &c.

def: s. f. (dakh.) The wandering without house and home.

s.f. corr. of dāmanī, q.v. s.f. Part of a woman's dress, a veil, a mantle; — a scrap of a shroud kept by the relations of a deceased person; a piece of cloth, inscribed with the genealogy of the priest of a deceased woman, which is placed on her shroud; — a saddle-cloth, furniture, housings; — an ornament worn on the forehead.
more: s. (dakh.) A womanʼs veil or mantle: see دامني dāman-ī above.

def: s.m. (f. ?), lit. 'Forest-enemy'; a fire that breaks out in a forest, a forest conflagration ( = davānal, q.v.s.v. dava, dav).
more: n. f. S. दाव forest, अनल fire. A burning forest.

n. m. An entrance; a door; gate; gateway. 1. Kahī nāhīṅ mānat, barjī nahīi mānat, Jab dekho tab duāre haigī khaṛī, Saiyāṅ, chhoṭī nanadyā hai jor-bharī . Farrukh. Wom. song. She will not heed me, brother, check her as I may, Look when you will she's standing at the door alway, The furor o'er my sister-in-law hath now full sway. 2. Kuār, jāṛā duār! Prov. September, door of winter! 3. Kāhe ko hai mere duāre ābat? Kyoṅ duāre kī ṭāṭyā khuṛkābat hai? Chhailā! kāhe ko man bhaṭkāvat hai! Farrukh Wom. song. Why hauntest thou my door thus o'er and o'er? Why dost thou knocking keep at my mat-door? O rakish beau! why thus thy heart afflictest sore? 4. Kāhe ko tum āwat ho saj sai, bālam, abhī more duāre,? Tumrī prit ham jānat haiṅ wā sautanyā ke duāre . Why, husband, deck yourself and come before my door? Your love I know full well is at my co-wife's door. duāre , prep. Through; by; by means of.
adj. (superl. of دور) Round, revolving. چرخ دوار charḵẖ-i-dauwār, The revolving heavens.
s. f. Fire that breaks out in woods.
wety: From Sanskrit द्वार (dvāra), from Proto-Indo-Iranian, from Proto-Indo-European *dʰworom (“enclosure, courtyard, i.e. something enclosed by the door”), from *dʰwor-, compare *dʰwer-.

def: s.m. = ḍābar, q.v. s.m. Low or hollow ground where water collects ( = ḍabrā and ḍahrā), a marsh, swamp, a pool, puddle, a small pond, pit, tank
subs: a vessel for washing the hands in
adj. f. 'Saucer-eyed,' large-eyed; — having eyes that quickly fill with tears, moist-eyed.

def: s.m. God; — a just prince; a sovereign; a judge, administrator, arbiter.
more: n. m. God; a sovereign. dāvar-i-mahshar , n. m. The God of resurrection. Kah deṅge ham yeh Dāvar-i-mahshar ke robrū, Ham ne gunāh kīye terī rahmat ke zor par . Before the God of resurrection I'll to him just say, I sinned because my hopes of pardon on God's mercy lay.

def: s.f. Sovereignty; empire, dominion.

ety: for A. دعوة, inf. n. of un. fr. دعو 'to pray, to call,' &c.
def: s.f. A prayer, benediction; a call, invitation, convocation, call or invitation to Mohammadanism; invitation to a repast or feast; fare, repast, feast, banquet; invocation (of spirits), exorcism; — pretension, claim
more: n. f. دعو calling. A feast; a treat (H. jeonār ). dāvat-i-islām , Forcing one to embrace Mahommedanism. dāvat-i-jaṅg , Proclamation of war. dāvat-i-samarqand , A feast one day, dry bread the next. dāvat-i-shīrāz ; H. dāl-dalyā . Simple fare; potluck. dāvat qubūl k . To accept an invitation to a feast. dāvat k., yā denā , v. a. To give a feast ( jimānā ). dāvat meṅ jānā , v. n. To go to a feast. dāvat-i-valīmā, tām-i-valīmā , Mah. n. f. A marriage feast.
s. f. 1. Invitation, convocation, benediction. 2. Pressing people to become Musalmāns. 3. Pretension. 4. A feast, banquet. دعوت کرني daﻌwat-karnī, To invite.
subs: s.f. 'Feast of Samarcand'; a feast one day and a fast the next
s.f. 'Fare of Shīrāz'; simple fare, a plain repast
(-kī), To accept an invitation to a feast
(-kī), To give a feast; to mvite
v.n. To go to a feast
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَعْوَت (da'wat), borrowed from Arabic دَعْوَة (daʕwa), from دَعَا (daʕā).

def: invitation card, invitation letter
wety: From Urdu دعوت (“invitation”) + Urdu نامہ (“letter”).

ety: for A. دواة, fr. دوي; see dawā
def: s.f. An ink-holder, inkstand; an inkhorn, a portable case with receptacles for ink and writing-reeds, &c.; — ink
more: n. f. 1. An inkstand. 2. ( Fac .) The vulvā; anus. Likheṅ hum aish kī takhī ko kis tarah, aë jāṅ! Qalam zamīn ke ūpar, davāt koṭhe par . Nazīr. The lines of pleasure on the page how trace, my love? The pen lies here below, the inkstand is above.
subs: s.f. The style with which the cloth or wool in an (Eastern) ink-bottle is stirred.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دوات (dawāt), from Arabic دَوَاة (dawāh). Compare Gujarati દવાત (davāt), Marathi दौत (daut), Punjabi ਦਵਾਤ (davāt) / دوات (doāt), Assamese দোৱাত (düat).

def: s.f. pl. (of daʻwat), Prayers, benedictions; convocations, invitations, &c.
direct plural of دَوات (davāt)
direct plural of دَعْوَت (da'vat, “invitation; invite”)

oblique plural of دَعْوَت (da'vat, “invitation; invite”)
v.t. To tread out corn, to thresh, to tread; — s.m. The act of treading corn.
more: v. a. To thrash, to tread.

more: n. m. دور turning round. See دور 4. daurān-i-khūn, sairān-i-khūn , Circulation of the blood. daurān-i-sar , n. m. A swimming in the head; dizziness; giddiness; vertigo.
(from دور daur ) s. m. 1. Time, age. 2. Fortune, vicissitude. 3. A revolution, period, a circle, cycle. دوران سر daurān-i-sar, s. m. A swimming in the head, dizziness, giddiness, vertigo.
subs: s.m. A swimming in the head, dizziness, giddiness, vertigo.

right (side)

more: s. f. A nurse, a midwife.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دایه (dāya).

def: s.f. A gift ( = daʼī).
more: n. f. S. दया from दा to give. 1. Sympathy; fellow-feeling; tenderness. 2. Pity ( tars , 2). 1. Krodh, manoj, lobh, mad, māyā! Chhūṭahīṅ sakal Rān kī dāyā . Rāmāyan. Ire, lust, pride, avarice, and false illusion's sway! All evil passions which Ram's grace can take away. 2. Dayā dharam kā mūl hai, pāp mūl abhimān, Tulsī! dayā na chh/?/ḍiye jab lag ghaṭ meṅ pirān . Religions's root compassion is, the root of sin is pride, Tulsī! ne'er pity be without while life in you doth bide. 2. Kindness; favor. āp kī dayā, yā kirpā hai! You are very kind! Thank you!

s. f. 1. A gift. 2. Affection, tenderness, sympathy, mercy, kindness, favour. ديا سيل दयाशील dayā-sīl, adj. Affectionate. ديا مان dayā-mān ( दयावान् ) or دياونت दयावन्तः dayā-want, adj. Charitable, merciful, kind.
wety: From Sanskrit दाय (dāya).

def: s.f. Compassion, pity, sympathy, tenderness, mercy, clemency, affection, love, favour, kindness, benevolence
subs: s.m. A pitiful or sympathetic person; a proper name
dayā-rūpī, adj. Of the nature, appearance, or form of compassion; compassionate, pitiful, merciful, tender, affectionate, kind, beneficent, generous, &c.
dayā-sindhu, s.m. 'Ocean of compassion, &c.' ( = dayā-nidhi, q.v.)
adj. (f. -ā), Connected with, or possessed of or endowed with, compassion, or mercy; compassionate, kind, &c.
or dayā-śīl, adj. (f. -ā), Of a compassionate disposition, compassionate, tender-hearted, merciful, affectionate, kind, &c.
(f. -ī), or dayā-kar (f. -ī), dayā-kr̤it (f. -ā), adj. Showing pity or compassion, sympathetic, pitiful, merciful, affectionate, kind, benevolent, beneficent, generous, liberal
adj. (f. matī), Compassionate, merciful, kind, &c. (see dayā-kar)
dayā-may, adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of, or abounding in, compassion, &c.
s.m. 'Treasure of mercy'; a very compassionate person; an epithet of the Deity
dayā-vat, dayā-van, or dayā-vant, adj. (f. vatī) = dayā-mān, q.v.
s.m. 'A hero of compassion,' a very compassionate or merciful person

adj. Merciful, compassionate, gracious, beneficent, kind.

def: s.m. Flying, flight (of a bird); — a car or litter carried upon men's shoulders, a palanquin, a 'doolie.'
n. m. دين became indebted. A creditor; an obliger.
more: n. f. S. डाकिनी. . 1. An old woman reputed to have an evil eye; a female goblin; a witch. 1. Mā ḍāyan ho to kyā bachchoṅ hī ko khāë? Prov. The goblin will not devour her own child. 2. Dāyan bhī das ghar chhoṛ-kar khātī hai . Prov. Even a goblin spares nine houses in ten. 3. Aisā jo mere sāth lagī jāëgī jhāṅpo, Ek roz mujhe ghar se nikalvāëgī jhāṅpo, Sab khā chukī mujh ko bhī ye ab khāëgī jhāṅpo, Woh kaun sā din hogā jo mar jāëgī jhāṅpo? Ab to mujhe ḍāyan sī nazar ātī hai buṛhyā . Nazīr. If like this always at me goes this harridan, Some day turned out I shall be by this harridan, When all's eaten up me will devour this harridan, When ever is she going to die this harridan? This old hag in my eyes now seems a limb of Hell? 4. Ḍāyan khāë to mūṅh lāl, na khāë to mūnh lāl . Prov. The goblin has a bloody mouth whether she devours human beings or not. (Give a dog a bad name and hang him). 2. A…

r: ḍāyan, ḍāyin, ḍāʼiṅ, ḍāʼeṅ, ḍākin, ḍākinī
def: s.f. A female goblin, a witch, an old hag who is reputed to have an evil eye; an ugly old woman.

s.m. corr. of dīwān, q.v. s.m. A royal court; a tribunal (of justice or revenue); a council of state, senate, divan; a court or hall of audience; — a minister, a chief officer of state; finance minister, head of the revenue or financial department; a secretary; a steward; — a complete series of odes or other poems by one author running through the whole alphabet (the rhymes of the first class terminating in alif, the second in be, and so on); — the collected writings of an author
subs: s.m. A prime minister, a vazīr
s.m. Accountant-general of the royal revenue, chancellor of the exchequer
s.m. A tribunal, office; a hall of audience, hall, court, chamber, a public room (detached from the house)

def: a region in Saudi Arabia comprising of the Holy cities of Mecca and Medina; the Holy Land (a region in the Middle East, consisting of Palestine and Israel, where Biblical events are set)
ex: دوسری جانب سعودی وزارت داخلہ نے مناسک حج کے آغاز سے چند گھنٹے پہلے ایک اعلان میں بتایا ہے کہ دیارِ مقدّسہ پہنچنے والے عازمین کی تعداد بیس لاکھ ہو چکی ہے۔

On the other hand, the Saudi Interior Ministry has announced in an announcement a few hours before the start of the Hajj rituals that the number of pilgrims who have reached the Holy Land has reached two million.
wety: From Classical Persian دیارِ مقدسه (diyār-i muqaddasa). By surface analysis, دَیَار (dayār, “land; region”) + مُقَدَّسَہ (muqaddasa, “Holy; scared”).

def: s.f. A goddess, &c. ( = devī, q.v.). s.f. A female deity, a goddess; the wife of a deity, (especially) Durgā or Bhawānī (wife of Śiva); — a queen; the plant Trigonella corniculata (and several other plants)
more: n. f. A goddess. 1. Debī pittar, mere peṭ bhītar . Prov. For goddess, ancestor the meal I should provide I'd rather have to fill my own inside. 2. Debī! tero bhagat bheṛyā ne khāyo, Hāth pām vāke b/?/hār nikse, Mūṅḍ bile meṅ pāyo! Rus. Bhajan. O Goddess, see! the wolf your worshipper hath eaten, Her feet and arms without, her head found in his den.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Left (hand, or side = dewrā; syn. bāyāṅ).

v.t. See s.v. denā. v.t. To give, grant, impart, yield, produce, afford, emit; to lay (eggs); to resign; to pay (a debt, &c.); to cause, occasion (as pain, &c.); to allow, let, permit (after an inflected infinitive, e.g. jāne denā); to place, put (on, or in or into, -meṅ; e.g. gale-meṅ hāth denā; pānī-meṅ zahr denā); to put (on), apply; to give forth, to raise (a cry for help, &c.); to close (a door); — s.m. Giving; paying; that which is to pay, debt, liability, &c.
subs: s.m. Assets and liabilities, debit and credit, profit and loss; settling (one's) affairs
v.t. To give away (de-ḍālnā); to make over, deliver, consign; to give up, resign, concede
v.t. To give away, to bestow
v.t. To dash down, to dash (against, -par), to throw; to stamp

(with acc.), To leave at death, to bequeath. (N.B. Not only is the root or the past conj. part. of denā prefixed to another verb to intensify or to modify its meaning, as in de-ḍālnā, de-marnā, &c.; but the verb itself is commonly added to the base of another verb to intensify its sense, e.g. thakā-denā, 'to tire out'; pheṅk-denā, 'to throw away': it is however frequently added without affecting the sense of the other verb; and such a compound is even more common than the simple verb.)

adj. & s.m. 'Sky-clothed'; having only the sky or atmosphere for raiment, clad by the regions of space, unclad, stark naked; — a naked person; a naked Hindū ascetic; an order of ascetics of the Jain sect who go naked (if clad in coloured cloth, they are called pītāmbar; if in white, svet-āmbar); a member of that order, a Jain mendicant; an epithet of Śiva (from his being naked), and of certain of his devotees
s.m. The end of space, the horizon, remote distance
s.m. Another region, a distant quarter; aërial space, the atmosphere
s.m. The functions of a dig-pāl or guardian deity of any region

dig-pati, s.m. The regent or guardian of one of the ten regions or points of the compass, or of a quarter of the world (of these guardian deities there are two divisions; 1˚ Astronomical, viz. the Sun, of the east; Saturn, of the west; Mercury, of the North; Mars, of the south; Venus, of the south-east; Rahū, of the south-west; the Moon, of the north-west; and Jupiter, of the north-east; — 2˚ Mythological, viz. Brahma, for upwards; Ananta, for downwards; Indra, of the east; Varuṇa, of the west; Kuvera, of the north; Yama, of the south; Agni, of the south-east; Nirriti, of the south-west; Marut or Vāyu, of the north-west; and Iʼśāna or Śiva, of the north-east)

dig-śūl, s.m. Any inauspicious planetary conjunction (as for the Sun and Venns to be in the west, &c.); see diśā-śūl, s.v. diśā; — dig-gaj, s.m. 'Elephant of the quarter'; a mythical elephant of one of the eight quarters or points (which, with others at different quarters, is supposed to support the world); (met.) great, large, huge
s.m. Point or quarter of the compass, point, quarter, direction
s.m. Subjugation of various countries in all directions; subjugation of extensive tracts of country (whether by arms or controversy), universal conquest
s.m. Invasion of various countries, going forth to conquer the whole world
s.m. Conqueror of many realms, or of the world

ety: dekh˚ = देक्ख(इ) = S. द्रक्ष्य(ति), the future of rt. दृश् (used in the sense of the present)
def: v.t. To see, look, look at, behold, view, observe, perceive, inspect, mark, note, consider, look to, weigh well, examine, prove, try; to search, scan; to watch (for); to feel (as the pulse, &c.); to experience, suffer, endure, tolerate, bear the sight of
more: v. a. S. दृश, Pr. avakkhaī pekkhā , he sees; Pālī, dīchchatī . 1. To see; look at; view; behold. 1. Dekhā na bhālā, 'sadqe gaī khālā! ' Prov. Nor seen nor known, 'bless you, my Aunt! 2. Nānhīṅ tā chhauṅṛā, ugnā nāoṅ, yeh gel, voh gel, dekhahu nā pāol . Tir. Riddle. Ans. Shīshe kī parchhāīṅ . The reflection of a mirror. A tiny shade just come, now have, now there, I could not find it anywhere. 3. Āo piyāre nain meṅ, palak ḍhāṅk tohe lūṅ! Nā maiṅ dekh/?/ṅ aur ko, nā tohe dekhan dūṅ . Dohā. Come, love, within these eyes! my eyelids shall a cover make, I will not look without, nor shall your eyes e'er me forsake. 4. Bole Shyām madhur ras batiyāṅ, tum sab lāj tajo rī, Lāj chhoṛ sanmakh sab āo, nakh sikh sab dekho rī! Holī song. Kanhaīya. Sūr Dās. Sham said in honeyed accents sweet, 'cast from you all your shame, Before me stand, from head to foot let me survey your frame!' 2. To observe; mark…

v. a. To see, to look at, view, observe, mark, inspect, behold, perceive, to experience. ديکهنا بهالنا dekhnā bhālnā, To see, to look at. ديکها ديکهي dekhā-dekhī, s. f. Emulation, competition, the looking at each other or being within sight (of objects).
subs: To see, look at, view, inspect, examine, try

v.t. To be permitted to see, to get to see; to find out, to ascertain
v.n. To be seen, to be or become visible, to appear; to seem

Not only is the root or the past conj. part. of denā prefixed to another verb to intensify or to modify its meaning, as in de-ḍālnā, de-marnā, &c.; but the verb itself is commonly added to the base of another verb to intensify its sense, e.g. thakā-denā, 'to tire out'; pheṅk-denā, 'to throw away': it is however frequently added without affecting the sense of the other verb; and such a compound is even more common than the simple verb.)

ety: de˚ = Prk. दे(इ) = S. ददा(ति), rt. दा, or S. दीय(ते), passive of दा

more: v. a. S. दा Pr. dāūṇā, diṇṇā ; P. dādan . 1. To give; confer; endow; impart; assign; allot; administer. 1. De na sake kāhu kachhā, mar gaë māyā joṛ, Chale gaë jā des se, jaise jābat chor . Dohā. 2. Chuglā baiṭhā nīm pe, de sāle ke tīn saë! Prov. The tale-bearer is up a tree, now pelt him freely we. 3. Jis ne dīyā us ne pāyā . Prov. Who gave (in a former birth) has received (in this). 4. De to bhalā, na de to bhalā! Beggars. Bless you if you give, bless you if you dont give! 5. Denā aur marnā barābar hai . Prov. As hard for him to give as it would be to cease to live. 2. To afford; yield; produce. 3. To pay; discharge; liquidate ( chukānā ). 4. To give in charity, as a gift, etc.; to offer. Sab se dīyā anūp hai, dīyā karo sab koë, Ghar meṅ dharā na pāīye jo kar dīyā na hoë . Dohā. 1. There's nothing like a lamp, his lamp let each one light, What's in the house you'll never find without li…
